title: Verify historical origin claim accuracy
abstract: The note states jazz piano emerged in New Orleans in the early 20th century. This is broadly accurate but could be contested; jazz piano as a distinct style developed across several cities. Consider adding nuance if precision matters.

title: Confirm pianist attributions are correct
abstract: Fats Waller, James P. Johnson (stride), Bud Powell (bebop), Bill Evans (modal), Cecil Taylor (free jazz), and Vijay Iyer and Robert Glasper (contemporary) are all correctly attributed to their respective substyles.

title: Free jazz and contemporary styles now included
abstract: The revised note covers free jazz (Cecil Taylor) and contemporary fusion styles (Vijay Iyer, Robert Glasper), addressing the previous omission flagged by the reviewer.

title: Left/right hand description removed
abstract: The inaccurate characterization of fixed left/right hand roles has been removed. The note no longer implies a rigid division of labor that does not reflect modern jazz piano practice.
